title: Counterfactual Stop-Now vs Stop-Later Features
summary: F1 strategy literature emphasizes undercut/overcut payoffs, tyre warm-up, pit-loss, and competitor interaction rather than raw tyre age alone.
rationale: F1 strategy literature emphasizes undercut/overcut payoffs, tyre warm-up, pit-loss, and competitor interaction rather than raw tyre age alone. A simple approximate cost-to-go margin can expose whether stopping now is strategically dominant even without a full simulator.
implementation_hint: Estimate race-level pit-loss and fresh-tyre warm-up penalty from train data, then create features like `cost(stop_now)-cost(stop_next_lap)`, `undercut_gain_est`, `overcut_risk_est`, and adjacent-position threat scores using same-lap rivals’ tyre-age/compound percentiles.
expected_effect: Should improve timing decisions near the pit window where static tyre-life ratios miss tactical context.
risk: No true gap-to-car data is provided, so payoff approximations may be noisy or circuit-specific.
